creator: me@svmhdvn.name
text:
Any progress on this? I think the issue may reach farther than just jails, as a lot of widely used packages have unintuitive syntax for specifying IPv6 link-local addresses.

For example, postfix (with the "lmtp_host_lookup = native" option set) does not accept 'fe80::abcd:1234%lo0' when set in /etc/hosts, but it DOES accept 'fe80:2::abcd:1234'. Users should not have to know the numeric scope index and interface creation order to specify IPv6 link-local addresses.

From the developer handbook at https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/developers-handbook/ipv6/#ipv6-scope-index

> Some of the userland tools support extended numeric IPv6 syntax, as documented in draft-ietf-ipngwg-scopedaddr-format-00.txt. You can specify outgoing link, by using name of the outgoing interface like "fe80::1%ne0". This way you will be able to specify link-local scoped address without much trouble.
>
> To use this extension in your program, you will need to use getaddrinfo(3), and getnameinfo(3) with NI_WITHSCOPEID.

Does this mean that the onus is on userland software to augment all their hosts-based name resolution with the NI_WITHSCOPEID? Or does freebsd have to default to NI_WITHSCOPEID set?

Also, I don't see NI_WITHSCOPEID anywhere in the freebsd src tree, does this functionality exist? Is it supposed to be NI_NUMERICSCOPE instead?
